subject: refactor(dashboard): Phase 4d-j — strip remaining custom classes
body:
Deletes every remaining custom CSS class from styles.css. The allowlist
is now empty — the vanilla-DaisyUI sweep is complete.

Deleted custom classes + migrations:

  .dashboard-header + ::after gradient — removed entirely. Header.jsx
    now uses `border-b border-base-300` for the divider. Decorative
    primary-tinted gradient line is gone.

  .hamburger-dropdown + @keyframes hamburger-fade-in — HamburgerMenu.jsx
    now uses inline Tailwind utilities for the dropdown surface:
    `fixed min-w-52 max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)] bg-base-200 border
    border-base-300 rounded-md shadow-xl z-50 py-1`. The 0.12s fade-in
    animation is gone; dropdown appears immediately.

  .hamburger-update-dot + @keyframes hamburger-pulse — Header.jsx now
    uses `w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary animate-pulse` (Tailwind's
    stock animate-pulse utility) for the PWA update indicator.

  .scrollbar-hide + ::-webkit-scrollbar — TabBar.jsx mobile tab row
    now shows the native scrollbar when overflowing. Minor aesthetic
    regression accepted per vanilla directive.

  .header-filter-hint — Header.jsx filtered-state button now uses
    DaisyUI `btn btn-link btn-sm p-0 min-h-0 h-auto text-primary`
    (native underlined link-style button).

  .root-error-message / .root-error-detail / .root-error-hint —
    main.jsx root error boundary fallback now inlines Tailwind
    utilities directly. If CSS load fails, the error message falls
    back to browser default styling. Acceptable given the vanilla-only
    directive — previously the root-error classes existed specifically
    to survive CSS failure.

  @media print block trimmed: removed references to deleted
    `.dashboard-header`, `.hamburger-menu`, `.header-filter-hint`.
    Only element selectors (body, a, section) and DaisyUI overrides
    remain. Print-hiding of chrome is handled via Tailwind's `print:hidden`
    variant on JSX consumers instead.

daisyui-surfaces.test.mjs:
  - LEGITIMATE_CUSTOM_CLASSES allowlist: set to empty Set — every
    custom class has been deleted. The allowlist guard now enforces
    a zero-custom-class policy.
  - No allowlist entries remain to verify against.

Visual regressions (all accepted per vanilla directive):
  - Header no longer has decorative gradient accent line below it
  - Hamburger dropdown appears without fade-in animation
  - Mobile tab bar shows native scrollbar when overflowing
  - Root error fallback has simpler typography

Verified: vite build clean (2.75s), 60/60 tests pass, allowlist is empty
and the guard test passes trivially.

Final custom-CSS count: 0.
Total custom classes deleted in the full vanilla sweep: 24 → 0.

Tags: refactor, ui, daisyui, cleanup, vanilla
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
